results.name: Login page renders
results.reasoning: The login page displays correctly with split-screen design: left panel shows Sportsphere branding, tagline 'The Sports Community Built for Athletes', feature list, and statistics (12K+ active athletes, 340 live streams, 890+ coaches). Right panel has working sign-in form with email/password fields, Sign In button, and Create Account option. Live stream widget shows real data (NBA Pre-season Training Analysis with 2.4K watching). All UI elements are properly rendered and functional.

results.name: Invalid credentials rejected
results.reasoning: Login failure is properly handled - error toast shows 'Incorrect email or password' at top, red error message appears below sign in button, and user remains on login page without being redirected to the app

results.name: Valid login redirects to Feed
results.reasoning: The authenticated app is working correctly - left sidebar shows complete navigation menu (Feed, Explore, Reels, ProPath, Live, Messages, Profile, Create Post), main feed displays real user stories at top, active post from Marcus Silva with soccer content and engagement options, proper dark theme throughout, and functional UI elements like Follow button and sport category filters

results.name: Session persists on refresh
results.reasoning: The feed page is properly loaded and functional after refresh. Shows authenticated user state with profile avatar in sidebar, populated stories section with user avatars and names, active feed filters (For You, Following, All, Basketball, Soccer, Football), a real post from Marcus Silva with proper engagement options and content, and sports news section. No login redirect or empty states detected.

results.name: Sign-up form has role selection
results.reasoning: The Create Account tab is active and properly displays the role selection interface with all 4 user type cards (Athlete, Coach, Organization, Parent) clearly visible with appropriate icons and descriptions. The form shows proper functionality with 'I am joining as a...' prompt and includes sign-in link for existing users.

results.name: Logout actually logs out
results.reasoning: Landing page displays correctly with proper dark theme, Sportsphere branding, tagline, and clear CTA buttons (Get Started/Sign In). No authenticated content is visible, confirming user is fully logged out as expected.

results.name: Auth guard blocks Feed when logged out
results.reasoning: Landing page displays correctly with proper dark theme, Sportsphere branding, descriptive text, and functional Get Started/Sign In buttons. This is the expected behavior for unauthenticated users being redirected from protected pages.

results.name: Sidebar renders all primary nav
results.reasoning: Left sidebar displays all 7 navigation items (Feed, Explore, Reels, ProPath, Live, Messages, Profile) with proper icons and labels. Feed is correctly highlighted in red as the active page, indicating proper navigation state management.

results.name: Navigate to Explore
results.reasoning: The Explore page is fully functional with working search bar, sport category filters (All, Baseball, Basketball, Football, Soccer, Tennis, Softball), and displays real training content including video thumbnails with durations, titles, tags, and engagement metrics. The page shows both Training & Drills and Popular Posts sections with actual content, not empty states or loading spinners.

results.name: Navigate to Reels
results.reasoning: L2 FAIL: The Reels page shows an empty state with only category labels (Soccer, Crossfit, Swimming, Baseball) and placeholder content with usernames (Marcus, Zara) but no actual video thumbnails or real video content visible. This appears to be a broken or empty state rather than functional reels with playable content.

results.name: Navigate to ProPath
results.reasoning: The ProPath page is functioning correctly, showing the proper onboarding state for new users who haven't set up their athletic profile yet. The page displays a trophy icon, clear heading 'Set Up Your ProPath Profile', descriptive text about creating a sport profile to unlock features, and a prominent 'Create Sport Profile' CTA button. This is the expected empty state flow, not an error condition.

results.name: Navigate to Live
results.reasoning: L2 FAIL: This is an empty state showing 'No one's live right now' with no actual streaming content or functionality demonstrated. The page shows only placeholder text encouraging users to go live, which indicates the live streaming feature is not currently working or populated with real data.

results.name: Navigate to Messages
results.reasoning: L2 FAIL: Messages page shows empty state with no conversations loaded. The right panel displays 'Select a conversation' placeholder with a chat bubble icon, indicating no message data is being shown. This is an empty state rather than a working feature with real data.

results.name: Navigate to Profile
results.reasoning: Profile page displays correctly with proper user data (Test Athlete profile, email, stats showing 0 followers/following/posts), functional buttons (Settings, Edit, Monetization, Logout), and proper empty states for Featured Highlights and My Sport Profiles sections with clear call-to-action prompts. Layout is intact and no errors visible.

results.name: More menu expands
results.reasoning: The More menu is properly expanded showing all secondary navigation items (For You, Get Noticed, The Vault, Challenges, Performance, Scouting Hub, Events, Forums) with clear icons and labels. The main feed content is still visible and functional with real sports posts, news articles with timestamps, and user interactions. The layout maintains proper hierarchy and spacing in the dark theme.

results.name: Navigate to Challenges (secondary)
results.reasoning: L2 FAIL: The page shows an empty state with 'No challenges found' message, which means the Challenges feature is not populated with actual data. While the UI elements loaded correctly, there are no real challenges to display, making this an empty state rather than a functional feature with working data.

results.name: Navigate to Forums (secondary)
results.reasoning: L2 FAIL: The forums page shows an empty state with 'No topics found. Be the first to start a discussion!' message, indicating no actual forum content is being displayed. While the page structure and UI elements are present, the core functionality of showing forum topics is not working - it's showing an empty state rather than real forum data.

results.name: Return to Feed (round-trip)
results.reasoning: Feed page is working properly with real content - stories section shows user profiles, post from Marcus Silva displays with proper metadata (14 hours ago, Soccer tag), sports news sidebar shows recent articles with timestamps, and all navigation elements are functional. The page demonstrates a complete working state with actual user-generated content and news data.

results.name: Feed loads with real posts
results.reasoning: Feed displays real sports content with actual posts from Marcus Silva about soccer, including meaningful text 'Every touch matters. Every rep builds muscle memory', proper timestamps (14 hours ago), interaction buttons, and a black and white soccer player image. The sports news sidebar also shows real ESPN articles with actual headlines and timestamps, demonstrating the feed is functioning with genuine data rather than placeholders.

results.name: Sport filter pills exist
results.reasoning: The horizontal row of sport filter pills is visible and functional, showing 'For You', 'Following', 'All', 'Basketball', 'Soccer', and 'Football' options with proper styling and icons. The pills appear to be properly implemented with clear sport names and are positioned correctly in the feed interface.

results.name: Sport filter actually filters
results.reasoning: Basketball filter is working correctly - the Basketball pill is highlighted in red/orange while other pills (Following, All, Soccer, Football) remain in default dark styling, and the visible post from Jordan Williams is properly tagged with 'Basketball' and shows basketball-related content about gym training

results.name: Clear filter restores all posts
results.reasoning: The feed is displaying posts from multiple different sports as expected. I can see posts covering NFL (Tua/Falcons, Titans WR Robinson), MLB (Pats' Barmore), NFL again (Super Bowl MVP Walker, Raiders deal), Golf (Rory's TPC arrival), and other sports news. The main post shows Marcus Silva's soccer content, and the sports news sidebar shows diverse content from ESPN NFL, ESPN Golf, etc. The filter has been successfully cleared and content from various sports is visible.

results.name: Like button works
results.reasoning: L2 FAIL: Cannot verify the like functionality worked. No post shows a clear active/filled heart button state or visible like count increment. The interface shows posts but without clear indication that a like action was successfully executed and the state updated accordingly.

results.name: Unlike restores state
results.reasoning: L2 FAIL: Cannot verify the unlike action worked - the like button state and count are not visible in the current view. The support chat overlay is covering the post interaction area where the like button and count would be displayed, making it impossible to confirm if the unlike functionality is working properly.

results.name: Post author links to profile
results.reasoning: The profile page displays Marcus Silva's complete profile information including avatar image, name (São Paulo, Brazil), follower/following counts (0/0), posts count (20), favorite sports (Soccer - Professional), professional details (Central Midfielder from São Paulo with 50+ appearances), and achievements list. This is clearly a different user's profile than the test account, showing real data and proper functionality.

results.name: News widget has content
results.reasoning: L2 FAIL: The screen shows only a loading spinner on a black background with no visible content, sport news widget, or any functional interface elements. This appears to be stuck in a loading state without displaying the expected sports news widget or feed functionality.
